mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 60% of triggered instances or signal loses predictive power when unemployment rises below 0.5pp.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, which increases corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in > 50% of cases or interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during late-cycle phases or 5-day forward returns show no significant correlation with industrial production growth.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid acceleration in consumer price inflation triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in > 65% of high-inflation trigger months or inflation fails to predict Fed policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, boosting consumer confidence and corporate earnings forecasts to drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Divergence between payroll growth and unemployment decline yields negative returns in > 55% of cases or interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding economic activity and corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth signals produce negative forward returns in > 50% of quarters or Rank IC falls below 0.01 across rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal contracting manufacturing activity and weakening economic momentum, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 60% of consecutive decline periods or signal loses predictive accuracy during supply-chain disruption events.
